generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance LiDARSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Retrofitted LiDAR sensor providing raw point cloud data for perception in the teleoperated vehicle. Used for object detection and tracking in ACC applications."
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudData
]

instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Aggregated sensor fusion system providing vehicle state data including speed, position, orientation, and IMU/GPS/odometry inputs."
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateData
]

instance MotorActuationSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "On-board sensor monitoring motor actuation signals for throttle and brake commands."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorActuationData
]

instance SteeringActuationSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "On-board sensor monitoring steering actuation commands."
    DTDFVocab:producedData SteeringActuationData
]

instance ServoActuationSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "On-board sensor monitoring servo actuation commands."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ServoActuationData
]

instance EnvironmentalContextSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor providing environmental context including projected driving path from ACC system."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnvironmentalContextData
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARSensor
    base:desc "Raw point cloud data transmitted from LiDAR sensor for clustering and tracking."
]

instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
    base:desc "Aggregated vehicle state data including speed, position, and orientation."
]

instance MotorActuationData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorActuationSensor
    base:desc "Motor actuation signals for throttle and brake commands."
]

instance SteeringActuationData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SteeringActuationSensor
    base:desc "Steering actuation commands."
]

instance ServoActuationData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ServoActuationSensor
    base:desc "Servo actuation commands."
]

instance EnvironmentalContextData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnvironmentalContextSensor
    base:desc "Environmental context including projected driving path from ACC."
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance PointCloudClusteringModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Computational model for clustering raw LiDAR point cloud data into meaningful object tracks (e.g., vehicles, obstacles)."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataPreProcessingEnabler
]

instance VehicleStateAggregationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Model aggregating sensor fusion data (IMU, GPS, odometry) into coherent vehicle state representation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataPreProcessingEnabler
]

instance ObjectTrackingModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Model tracking detected objects (e.g., lead vehicles) over time for trajectory prediction."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataPreProcessingEnabler
]

instance LiDARPointCloudDataProcessed : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed LiDAR data transformed into clustered object tracks (e.g., vehicle state, obstacle tracks, driving paths)."
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationEnabler, DataStorageEnabler
]

instance VehicleStateDataProcessed : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed vehicle state data including speed, position, and orientation after sensor fusion."
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationEnabler, DataStorageEnabler
]

instance ActuationCommandData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Validated actuation commands (motor, steering, servo) before transmission to physical actuators."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorActuationData, SteeringActuationData, ServoActuationData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ActuationFeedbackEnabler
]

instance EnvironmentalContextDataProcessed : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed environmental context including projected driving path for ACC decision-making."
    DTDFVocab:fromData EnvironmentalContextData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationEnabler, DataStorageEnabler
]

instance HistoricalVehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Logged historical vehicle state data for post-hoc analysis by developers and operators."
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateDataProcessed
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataLoggingEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance DataPreProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Functional entity performing point cloud clustering, tracking, and vehicle state aggregation from raw sensor data."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance DataTranslationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Ensures serialization, message type alignment, and unit conversions for all data streams between entities."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance DataStorageEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Persistent storage for processed data (e.g., object tracks, vehicle state) with retrieval capabilities."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, DataLoggingService
]

instance DataLoggingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Generates reports and logs historical vehicle state data for analysis."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance ActuationFeedbackEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Transmits computed desired speed commands back to on-board actuators (throttle, brakes)."
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance CloudProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "External cloud-based infrastructure handling computationally intensive ACC tasks (lead vehicle detection, trajectory planning)."
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance ROS2CommunicationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS2 inter-node communication protocol for real-time data exchange between software components (e.g., Point Cloud Clustering, Data Translation)."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance WiFiCommunicationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Wi-Fi (UDP transport layer) for inter-entity communication between Physical Twin and Digital Twin with low-latency requirements."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance CustomDashboardEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Custom-developed user interface for real-time data visualization, report generation, and remote management of ACC."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, RemoteManagementService
]

// C6: Services
instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Provides real-time visualization of vehicle state, object tracks, and driving paths through the custom dashboard."
    DTDFVocab:provides VehicleStateInsight, ObjectTrackInsight, DrivingPathInsight, DataLoggingAction
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Offloads ACC computation (lead vehicle detection, speed trajectory planning) from on-board ECUs to cloud infrastructure."
    DTDFVocab:provides ACCComputationInsight, ActuationCommandAction
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Enables remote control of ACC (enable/disable, set speeds, emergency braking) with low-latency communication."
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemControlInsight, EmergencyBrakingAction
]

instance DataLoggingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Logs and reports historical vehicle state data for post-hoc analysis by developers and operators."
    DTDFVocab:provides HistoricalDataInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance VehicleStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization of vehicle speed, acceleration, and sensor inputs for operator awareness."
]

instance ObjectTrackInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Display of detected obstacles and lead vehicles in proximity for situational awareness."
]

instance DrivingPathInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Visualization of projected driving path based on ACC trajectory planning."
]

instance ACCComputationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Computed lead vehicle detection and distance-based desired speed for ACC decision-making."
]

instance SystemControlInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Current state of ACC system (enabled/disabled) and remote control capabilities."
]

instance HistoricalDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Access to logged historical vehicle state data for analysis and debugging."
]

instance ActuationCommandAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Transmission of computed desired speed commands to on-board actuators."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance DataLoggingAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic generation and saving of vehicle state reports."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance EmergencyBrakingAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Remote issuance of emergency braking commands."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance ThrottleActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "On-board actuator receiving desired speed commands from Digital Twin for throttle control."
]

instance BrakeActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "On-board actuator receiving emergency braking commands from Digital Twin or remote operator."
]

// C1: Entities
instance PhysicalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Physical representation of the 1/10-scale autonomous vehicle with resource-constrained ECUs and advanced sensors."
    base:isContainedIn ACCSystemEnvironment
]

instance DigitalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Centralized digital representation managing data collection, processing, and control mechanisms for ACC."
    base:isContainedIn ACCSystemEnvironment
]

instance UserEntity : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "User-facing interface for monitoring and control of ACC system."
    base:isContainedIn ACCSystemEnvironment
]

instance DataCollectionAndDeviceControlEntity : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Entity handling real-time data acquisition and actuation commands between Physical Twin and Digital Twin."
    base:isContainedIn ACCSystemEnvironment
]

// C16: Environment
instance ACCSystemEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Closed-loop autonomous driving environment for 1/10-scale vehicle with real-time monitoring and remote management requirements."
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **virtual-to-physical interaction** in this Digital Twin System (DTS) involves the transmission of control commands from the **Digital Twin Entity** (e.g., *Controlling FE*) to the **Physical Twin Entity** (e.g., motor, steering, servo actuators) via validated and authenticated control signals. The process is governed by the **Actuation FE** and **Controlling FE**, which generate and execute commands based on processed digital twin data (e.g., adaptive cruise control (ACC) logic).

**Command Types**:
- **Motor Actuation**: Speed control commands derived from ACC logic.
- **Steering Servo Actuation**: Steering angle adjustments based on path deviation or obstacle avoidance.
- **Vehicle State Overrides**: Emergency braking or corrective actions triggered by digital twin alerts.

**Control Message Formats**:
- Standardized message formats aligned via the **Data Translation FE**, ensuring compatibility between ROS2 and UDP/Wi-Fi communication layers.
- Commands include structured payloads for actuation parameters (e.g., torque, angle, duration).

**Validation and Authentication Mechanisms**:
- **Data Translation FE** performs unit conversions and message validation to ensure command integrity.
- Implicit validation via **Controlling FE** (e.g., ACC logic checks for feasibility before issuing commands).
- No explicit mention of cryptographic authentication, but **UDP over Wi-Fi** suggests potential for IP-based access control or firewall rules.

**Feedback Systems**:
- **Closed-loop control**: Actuation FE receives feedback from physical sensors (e.g., motor encoder data, steering position sensors) to adjust commands dynamically.
- **Real-time visualization** (Fig. 3a) provides operators with feedback on command execution (e.g., vehicle trajectory alignment with projected path).

**Conflict Resolution**:
- **Priority-based arbitration**: Critical commands (e.g., emergency braking) override non-critical adjustments (e.g., ACC speed adjustments).
- **Controlling FE** resolves conflicts by re-evaluating sensor data and digital twin state before reissuing commands.

**Command Execution Confirmation**:
- Implicit via sensor feedback loops (e.g., motor/steering sensors confirm execution).
- **Reporting FE** generates logs for post-hoc verification of command execution and system behavior.
"
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin System (DTS) enforces a **100 ms time synchronization accuracy** between physical and digital components to enable computation offloading and coordination among devices. This temporal granularity is dictated by hardware limitations, though stricter synchronization (sub-100 ms) is mandated by safety-critical vehicular standards (e.g., ISO 23247). The system leverages **clock synchronization mechanisms** to align distributed components, ensuring deterministic latency for real-time services such as sensor data processing, actuation, and device communication. The **Physical Twin Entity** and **Digital Twin Entity** operate under this unified time scale, with ROS2-based nodes executing time-sensitive functionalities (e.g., *Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking*) within the constrained window. No explicit mention of service-specific time scales or adaptive latency requirements is provided beyond this baseline synchronization constraint."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DTS orchestrates components using a **hybrid architecture pattern** rooted in **ISO 23247 RA**, where software components are **mapped to Functional Entities (FEs)** and deployed as **ROS2 packages** on distributed hardware. Key orchestration mechanisms include:
  1. **Entity-Based Decomposition**: Components are grouped into *Physical Twin*, *Digital Twin*, and *User Entities*, each hosted on separate hardware, enabling **modular resource management** (e.g., compute, sensors).
  2. **ROS2 Node Integration**: Each FE is implemented as a **ROS2 package** containing one or more nodes, facilitating **loosely coupled, message-passing coordination** via topics/services.
  3. **Deviations from ISO 23247**: The *Physical Twin Entity* extends beyond the standard’s *OME* by hosting **general automotive functionalities** (e.g., *Point Cloud Clustering*), requiring **custom ownership models** for engineering processes.
  4. **Selective FE Adoption**: Not all ISO 23247 FEs are implemented; the *User Interface FE* is split into *3D Visualization* and *Control Panel* subcomponents, while *Data Storage* lacks a direct FE alignment, necessitating **ad-hoc integration**.
  System-wide coordination is achieved through **ROS2’s middleware** (e.g., DDS for real-time communication) and **hardware-level synchronization** (100 ms clock accuracy), with no explicit mention of dynamic resource allocation or federated learning."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **technical network infrastructure** of this Digital Twin System (DTS) is designed as a hybrid architecture combining **ROS2 (Robot Operating System 2)** for intra-entity communication and **Wi-Fi (UDP transport layer)** for inter-entity communication between the Physical Twin, Digital Twin, and User entities.

**Communication Protocols**:
- **ROS2**: Used for software component communication within the Digital Twin Entity (e.g., *Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking* → *Data Translation* → *Visualization*). ROS2 provides:
  - Topic-based publish-subscribe model for real-time data exchange.
  - Service-based RPC for request-response interactions (e.g., command validation).
  - Quality-of-Service (QoS) profiles to prioritize latency-sensitive data (e.g., LiDAR tracks for ACC).
- **Wi-Fi (UDP)**: Used for inter-entity communication (Physical Twin → Digital Twin → User) with:
  - **UDP transport layer** for low-latency, high-throughput data streams (e.g., sensor data, control commands).
  - No explicit mention of TCP or reliability mechanisms, implying potential packet loss tolerance for non-critical data.

**Network Topology and Architecture**:
- **Star topology** for Wi-Fi communication, with the Digital Twin Entity acting as a central hub for data aggregation and distribution.
- **ROS2 network** operates as a decentralized graph where components communicate directly via topics/services.

**Bandwidth and Latency Requirements**:
- **LiDAR Data**: High bandwidth (≥100 Mbps) due to raw point cloud resolution; pre-processing reduces payload size.
- **Vehicle State/Actuation**: Low-latency (<50ms) for real-time control (e.g., ACC adjustments).
- **Visualization/Reporting**: Moderate bandwidth for 3D rendering and log storage.

**Reliability and Redundancy Mechanisms**:
- **ROS2 QoS profiles** ensure critical data (e.g., collision avoidance) is prioritized.
- **Data Translation FE** includes error handling for malformed messages or unit mismatches.
- No explicit redundancy (e.g., backup Wi-Fi links or ROS2 nodes), but **UDP retransmission** may occur at the application layer.

**Security Protocols**:
- **Wi-Fi Security**: Implicit reliance on standard Wi-Fi encryption (e.g., WPA3) for inter-entity communication.
- **ROS2 Security**: No explicit mention of authentication/authorization, but **topic access control** can be configured via ROS2 security plugins (e.g., TLS for sensitive commands).
- **Data Integrity**: Ensured by **Data Translation FE**’s message validation and unit conversion checks.
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The **hosting and deployment infrastructure** of this Digital Twin System (DTS) is not explicitly detailed in the provided documents, but inferred components and constraints can be derived from the architecture and service descriptions.

**Hosting Platforms**:
- **Edge Deployment**: Likely dominant due to real-time requirements (e.g., LiDAR processing, ACC control). Components like *Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking* and *Actuation FE* require low-latency execution near sensors.
- **On-Premise**: Digital Twin Entity and User Interface components (e.g., *3D Visualization*, *Reporting FE*) may reside on onboard computing units (e.g., vehicle ECUs or a central cockpit PC).
- **Cloud**: Not mentioned, but possible for non-real-time tasks (e.g., historical data storage, machine learning model training).

**Computational and Storage Requirements**:
- **Computational**:
  - **LiDAR Processing**: High-performance CPU/GPU for real-time point cloud clustering (e.g., NVIDIA Jetson or Intel Xeon).
  - **ROS2 Network**: Moderate CPU for message routing and QoS management.
  - **Visualization**: GPU-accelerated rendering for 3D cockpit displays.
- **Storage**:
  - **Data Storage FE**: Persistent storage for logs, reports, and historical sensor data (e.g., SSD/HDD for onboard systems).
  - **Volatile Storage**: RAM for real-time buffers (e.g., LiDAR point clouds, vehicle state).

**Scalability Mechanisms**:
- **Modular ROS2 Components**: Individual services (e.g., *Controlling FE*, *Reporting FE*) can be scaled independently by adding nodes or increasing computational resources.
- **Dynamic Topic Subscription**: ROS2 allows runtime adjustment of data flow priorities (e.g., reducing LiDAR resolution during non-critical phases).

**Deployment Models Supported**:
- **Monolithic (Onboard)**: All components deployed on a single vehicle ECU or cockpit PC for deterministic latency.
- **Distributed (Edge-Cloud Hybrid)**: Potential future extension to cloud for non-real-time analytics, but not described in the document.

**Availability/Reliability Assurance**:
- **Redundancy**: Not explicitly described, but **ROS2 QoS** and **Data Translation FE** provide resilience to transient failures.
- **Fault Tolerance**: Implicit via sensor feedback loops (e.g., if a command fails, the system re-evaluates via *Controlling FE*).
- **High Availability**: Critical for safety-critical applications (e.g., ACC), but no explicit HA clustering or failover mechanisms are mentioned.
"
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adheres to the **ISO 23247 standard for Digital Twin frameworks in manufacturing**, specifically **Part 2: Reference Architecture**, which defines an **entity-based structure** with four core entities: **Observable Manufacturing Element (OME)**, **Data Collection and Device Control Entity**, **Digital Twin Entity**, and **User Entity**. Each entity is composed of **modular Functional Entities (FEs)** responsible for discrete tasks (e.g., data transformation, actuation). The standard does not mandate specific **interoperability protocols**, but **OPC Unified Architecture (OPC UA)** is cited as a potential implementation choice for data exchange. **Certification requirements** or **compliance frameworks** beyond ISO 23247 are not referenced. The architecture ensures **interoperability** through standardized entity interactions, though no additional industry standards (e.g., IEC 62264, NIST frameworks) are mentioned."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system, particularly in the context of **ISO 26262-1:2018** (Road Vehicles – Functional Safety), must adhere to **functional safety standards** for safety-critical systems, including **automotive applications** (e.g., adaptive cruise control as referenced in **ISO 15622:2018**). This involves:
  - **Risk-based safety lifecycle management** (per **ISO 26262-1**), requiring **hazard analysis and risk assessment (HARA)** to classify safety goals (ASIL levels A-D) and allocate safety requirements to system components.
  - **Cybersecurity protocols** aligned with **V2X (Vehicle-to-Everything) services** (per **3GPP TS 22.185 v15.0.0**), including **authentication, authorization, and secure communication** for data integrity and confidentiality in distributed Digital Twin environments.
  - **Fail-safe mechanisms** (e.g., **watchdog timers, redundant systems**) to ensure graceful degradation or shutdown in case of failures, as mandated by **ISO 26262-1** for safety-critical automotive systems.
  - **Access control mechanisms** (e.g., **role-based access, cryptographic authentication**) to restrict unauthorized modifications to the Digital Twin model, particularly in **interactive cockpit applications** (as discussed in **Dalibor et al., 2020**), where real-time data integrity is critical.
  - **Threat mitigation strategies** for **cyber-physical attacks** (e.g., spoofing, replay attacks) in **V2X communication**, requiring **end-to-end encryption** and **message authentication codes (MACs)** as per **3GPP TS 22.185**.
  - **Model-driven safety validation** (per **Dalibor et al., 2020**), where **formal methods** (e.g., **UML/SysML models**) are used to verify safety properties of the Digital Twin’s **interactive cockpit** against **ISO 26262** compliance.
  The system must also integrate **real-time monitoring** of safety-critical parameters (e.g., sensor data, actuator states) to detect deviations and trigger **corrective actions** (e.g., system reset, failover) as per **ISO 15622** for adaptive cruise control."
]
